methods.description: Simulations were carried out using the Flight 1.25 model to test the effect of changes in the density of the flight medium on various flight parameters such as cruising airspeed, climb rate, and myofibril work.

steps.description: The input data used the above mass, wingspan and wing shape (AR) values.
steps.searchable_summary: use mass, wingspan, and wing shape values as input data for simulation

steps.description: Simulations were carried out with the widely used flapping model Flight 1.25.
The output of these simulations gives cruising airspeed at minimum power; climb rate at maximum power and myofibril work.
steps.searchable_summary: process simulation output to obtain cruising airspeed, climb rate, and myofibril work

methods.description: Analysis of takeoff speed using frame-by-frame video analysis and theoretical calculations based on Borelli's Rule and kinetic energy considerations.

steps.description: A frame by frame analysis of multiple videos of storks taking flight shows that they follow the pattern described in the literature of rapid leg extension, together with a strong wing upstroke.
steps.searchable_summary: perform frame-by-frame video analysis of stork takeoff

steps.description: Borelli's Rule was confirmed by Newton who reasoned that to jump to a given height (h) in a gravitational field (g), an animal must achieve a vertical takeoff velocity (v), which corresponds to kinetic energy.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Borelli's Rule to calculate vertical takeoff velocity

methods.description: Determination of air densities or paleo air pressure using two independent proxies: resin/amber and the difference between marine and terrestrial derived pCO2.

steps.description: To determine the air densities -or paleo air pressure -to be tested, two independent proxies were adopted: resin/amber and the difference between marine and terrestrial derived pCO2.
steps.searchable_summary: use resin/amber and pCO2 difference as proxies to determine air densities
